WOP-P Practicum · WANT Research Team · Universitat Jaume I

Some places study healthy work. I spent a semester inside one.

A reflective case study of my internship at the WANT Research Team — the people, the research, and what it taught me about being a scientist and a practitioner at once.

The memory libraryWhy this exists
scroll ↓
01

Why this exists

I could have written my Practicum up as a list of tasks. I didn't want to.

Being placed inside a group that is both a research lab and an applied consultancy raised a question I wanted to think through — can a research-heavy internship still make you a practitioner? This is that reflection, in a form you can move through.

02

The memory library

A research team is also a culture. What I'll carry home isn't a deliverable — it's being welcomed in my own language on day one, the lunches, the campus, the people. Tap any photo.

Click a photo to open it.

03

The song the team made

We asked an AI to write our team a song.

The point isn't that AI wrote it — it's what it's about: us. Technology in service of human connection, not instead of it. That, in one playful artifact, is the whole argument of my internship.

↓ download the song
04

The research, in one chapter

The centre of my placement was a meta-analysis: does good leadership buffer the dark side of AI? Here's the idea you can play with — and the tools I tested to build it.

Try it · the buffer hypothesis

Add leadership. Watch the outcomes shift.

AI demand aloneLeadership as resource
Work engagement—
Burnout—

Part of the work was evaluating the AI tools themselves — not whether to use AI, but how to use it critically. Tap a column to rank them.

Screening followed PRISMA 2020; nine focal studies anchor the pilot synthesis. Qualitative appraisal from my own benchmarking notes — a dated snapshot.

Read the full report (D5)

Keep the D5 file beside this page to enable the link.

05

How I reflected

To keep the reflection rigorous I ran it through Kolb's experiential-learning cycle. Tap each stage.

06

A small piece of real evidence

I ran an anonymous pulse with the team (n = 6) — a micro-mirror of the meta-analysis. Small and illustrative, but the pattern is telling.

4.3
Work
engagement
3.7
AI
pressure
4.4
AI
productivity
4.1
Leadership
as resource
4.3
Critical-use
climate

n = 6 · item means on a 1–5 scale · the demand (AI pressure) is the lowest bar; the resources are the highest — the buffered pattern the study argues for.

What I carry home

The next WOP psychologist will be judged not by whether they use AI, but by whether they can critically integrate it — keeping human wellbeing as the non-negotiable end.

ANIQA ISRAR · EMJMD WOP-P · COURSE 2025-26
